---
title: "Hypothesis Testing"
subtitle: "Interrogating the KG when you arrive with a prior"
---

## Overview {#sec-hypothesis}

This chapter is for users who arrive at the KG with a **prior**—a gene of interest from a paper, a pathway hypothesis, or a specific biological mechanism they want to test. The KG lets you rapidly check how much evidence exists for any prior across all analytical layers.

---

## I Have a Gene — What Does the KG Know?

Use this multi-layer gene audit as your default starting point whenever you have a gene of interest.

::: {.query-box}
Tell me everything the KG knows about gene HSPA5: differential expression, WGCNA module, MOFA weight, phenotype correlations, and published evidence.
:::

The KG will return evidence from:

1. <span class="evidence-badge badge-deg">DEG/DAA</span> Is it DE in high vs. low producers? In how many cohorts? In which direction?
2. <span class="evidence-badge badge-wgcna">WGCNA</span> Which module does it belong to? What is its kME? Is the module correlated with qP/titre/IVCD?
3. <span class="evidence-badge badge-mofa">MOFA</span> Does it have a significant Factor 1 weight? Positive or negative?
4. Spearman correlation with qP, titre, and IVCD directly.
5. <span class="evidence-badge badge-lit">Literature</span> Has it been manipulated in CHO cells? With what effect?

---

## I Have a Pathway — Which Genes in it Are Productivity-Associated?

::: {.query-box}
Which genes in the "Protein processing in endoplasmic reticulum" KEGG pathway are differentially expressed in BMS Study 1 high vs. low producer cohorts?
:::

::: {.query-box}
Show all genes in the glycolysis / gluconeogenesis pathway with positive Spearman correlation to qP in Study 1.
:::

---

## I Have a Gene List — Check Against All Evidence Layers

::: {.query-box}
I have a list of genes: [HSPA5, DNAJB9, MERTK, FICD, ZCCHC24]. For each, show: (1) DEG status in high vs. low Nivo producers, (2) WGCNA module, (3) MOFA Factor 1 weight, (4) literature evidence.
:::

---

## I Have a Phenotype — What Genes Are Most Correlated?

::: {.query-box}
Which genes have the strongest positive Spearman correlation with specific productivity (qP) in Study 1?
:::

::: {.query-box}
Rank proteins by their Spearman correlation with titre in Study 1, descending.
:::

::: {.query-box}
Which metabolites are most negatively correlated with qP?
:::

---

## Testing Specific Biological Hypotheses

### ER Stress / Unfolded Protein Response (UPR) Hypothesis

*Prior:* High producers activate UPR to expand ER folding capacity.

::: {.query-box}
Show all genes involved in the UPR or ER stress response that are upregulated in high producers in Study 1.
:::

::: {.query-box}
Is the "protein processing in endoplasmic reticulum" KEGG pathway enriched in the pro-productivity WGCNA module M1?
:::

::: {.query-box}
Which UPR-related transcription factors (ATF4, ATF6, XBP1) are differentially expressed in high producers?
:::

**KG verdict:** ✅ Strongly supported. Module M1 is enriched for ER processing / UPR. HSPA5, DNAJB9, and FICD carry positive MOFA Factor 1 weights. Literature evidence (PMID 32624757, 24081924) corroborates GRP78/BiP overexpression as a productivity lever.

---

### Secretory Pathway Hypothesis

*Prior:* High producers have enhanced protein secretion machinery.

::: {.query-box}
Are genes in the "protein export" and "antigen processing" pathways upregulated in high producers in Study 1?
:::

::: {.query-box}
Show the top co-expressed genes with HSPA5 in WGCNA module M1.
:::

**KG verdict:** ✅ Supported. Protein export and antigen processing are both enriched in WGCNA M1 (pro-productivity module).

---

### Metabolic Bottleneck Hypothesis (Lactate / Ammonia / ROS)

*Prior:* Runaway lactate accumulation limits productivity in low producers.

::: {.query-box}
Which metabolites significantly differ between high and low producers in Study 1? Highlight lactate.
:::

::: {.query-box}
Show genes in the lactate metabolism pathway (KEGG) with negative Spearman correlation to titre.
:::

::: {.query-box}
Are there published interventions (gene KO/OE) that reduce lactate accumulation in CHO cells?
:::

---

### Chaperone / Difficult-to-Express (DTE) Hypothesis

*Prior:* DTE proteins benefit from co-expression of specific chaperones.

::: {.query-box}
Show all chaperone genes (HSP family, DNAJ family) upregulated in high producers in Study 1.
:::

::: {.query-box}
What publications link chaperone overexpression to improved productivity of difficult-to-express proteins?
:::

---

### Apoptosis-Resistance Hypothesis

*Prior:* Blocking apoptosis extends culture longevity and increases total titre.

::: {.query-box}
Are anti-apoptotic genes (BCL2, BCL-XL family) upregulated in high producers in any BMS cohort?
:::

::: {.query-box}
Which published studies link apoptosis-resistance interventions (combined knockouts) to reduced lactate and improved productivity?
:::
